description_long: This is a concrete jersey barrier, commonly used for traffic control and pedestrian safety in urban environments. The barrier is rectangular with a slightly sloped top and rough, chipped edges, indicating exposure to outdoor elements and frequent use. Its surface is predominantly painted yellow, with a white base and visible areas of wear and weathering. One side features prominent graffiti in black and magenta, including text and a simple crown drawing, while the opposite side has smaller, less conspicuous black graffiti. The texture is rough and uneven, with visible cracks and chipped paint, especially along the top and base. The object appears solid and heavy, designed to resist movement and provide a physical obstacle.
description: A weathered yellow and white concrete jersey barrier with graffiti and chipped edges, showing signs of outdoor use and wear.
description_view_0: Front view showing yellow paint, white base, and large magenta graffiti with a crown drawing.
description_view_1: Angled view highlighting the rough, chipped top edge and the continuation of graffiti along the side.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying yellow paint, white base, and smaller black graffiti with less visible wear.
description_view_3: Angled rear view showing chipped edges, rough surface, and black graffiti on the yellow paint.
